entry.title: NY Times: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Poland Shows the Risks for Women When Abortion Is Banned
  • ‘Vegan Leather’: How Fashion Giants Recast Plastic as Good for the Planet
  • Momentum in Ukraine Is Shifting in Russia’s Favor
  • Jan. 6 Committee Appears to Lay Out Road Map for Prosecuting Trump
  • Pence Aide Warned Against Blocking Electoral College Count, Memo Shows
  • The Main Draw at Andrew Giuliani’s Campaign Events? His Father.
  • Coming From Separate Worlds in Taiwan, They Collided at California Church
  • In Uvalde, a Bishop Makes an Impassioned Plea: Tighten Gun Laws
  • Kelsie Whitmore Catches On With Staten Island FerryHawks
  • This Is No Way to Run a City (or a Country)
  • Donald Trump, American Monster
  • Why the Memory of Jan. 6 Can’t Prevent a Trump Resurgence
  • Where Is Dom Phillips?
  • If You Must Point Fingers on Inflation, Here’s Where to Point Them
  • Why Strangers Are Good for Us
  • Subsidized Ocean Fishing Threatens the Sea’s Bounty
  • The Scene at the Border
  • The Sunday Read: ‘The “E-Pimps” of OnlyFans’
  • A Caravan of Migrants Is Heading Toward the U.S. Border
  • What to Know About France’s Parliamentary Elections
  • Traffic-Clogged North England Bitter Over Stalled Rail Plan
  • ‘We Buried Him and Kept Walking’: Children Die as Somalis Flee Hunger
  • A Restored Painting Recalls the Colosseum’s Christian Past
  • Live Updates: Russia Could Soon Encircle Ukrainian Troops in Sievierodonetsk
  • China Won’t Hesitate to Fight for Taiwan, Defense Minister Warns
  • Rumbling Through Modern Jordan, a Railway From the Past
  • Newly United, French Left Hopes to Counter President in Upcoming Vote
  • Protests Over Prophet Muhammad Comments Turn Deadly in India
  • Biden Races to Expand Coalition Against Russia but Meets Resistance
  • McDonald’s Is Reinvented in Russia as the Economy Stumbles On
  • U.S. Warns China on Taiwan as Defense Officials Meet to Cool Tensions
  • U.S. Accelerated Expulsions of Haitian Migrants in May
  • A Radical Collective Takes Over One of the World’s Biggest Art Shows

  • Dozens of White Supremacists Arrested in Idaho Had Planned to Riot, Authorities Say
  • A Trove of Guantánamo Photos
  • Peter Lamborn Wilson, Advocate of ‘Poetic Terrorism,’ Dies at 76
  • Woman Gets 5-Year Ban From Grand Teton for False Report on Missing Hiker
  • A Secret City With a Secret African American History
  • Why Students Are Choosing H.B.C.U.s: ‘4 Years Being Seen as Family’
  • Should Biden Run in 2024? Democratic Whispers of ‘No’ Start to Rise.
  • The Dream of the Open Road Collides With the Reality of $5-a-Gallon Gas
  • More Retailers Look to Manufacture Excitement With Product ‘Drops’
  • The Week in Business: Trouble for Tesla?
  • When You’re Forced to Cash Out in a Bearlike Market
  • Howard Schultz: Starbucks Is Battling for the ‘Hearts and Minds’ of Workers
News from the New York Times

entry.title: Washington Post: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukraine says Severodonetsk chemical plant under attack; street fighting rages
  • China calls U.S. a ‘bully,’ vows to ‘fight to the end’ for Taiwan
  • Israel infighting could strip legal protections from West Bank settlers
  • In Kherson, misery under Russian occupation, hope over Ukrainian gains
  • Russia likely to seize all of Luhansk in coming weeks, U.S. official says
  • Russia controls most of Severodonetsk, governor says
  • Bolivia sentences ex-president to 10 years in prison after coup trial
  • Biden says Zelensky ‘didn’t want to hear’ U.S. warnings of invasion
  • Renaissance synagogues being restored in Venice's ghetto
  • Hot air brings Spain its 1st withering heat wave of year
  • UN-brokered talks on Libya elections resume in Cairo
  • 9 arrested after vicious assault on women in north China
  • Pakistan military: Soldier killed in shootout with militants
  • Venezuelan opposition leader attacked during national tour
  • Pair's disappearance in Brazil's Amazon tied to 'fish mafia'
  • Mexico issues 7,000 temporary documents to migrants in south
  • UN says concerned over Libya clashes, urges efforts for calm
  • Man killed in floods in Turkey's capital
  • Chris Smalls’s Amazon uprising and the fight for a second warehouse
  • 31 tied to hate group charged with planning riot near LGBTQ event in Idaho
  • The shadow race is underway for the Republican presidential nomination
  • Man arrested for leaving flowers on fiancee’s grave found guilty of littering
  • How the 3D-printing community worldwide is aiding Ukraine
  • Threat to Kavanaugh Will Irrevocably Change Justices’ Lives
  • Australia’s Early Flu Season Shows Americans Need Their Shots
  • US Hurricane Season Will Be Felt Around the World This Year
  • Boris Johnson’s Thatcherite Housing Ideas Won’t Go Very Far
  • The sedition didn’t stop on Jan. 6. It must be stopped.
  • $5 gas is largely Putin’s fault
  • The San Francisco district attorney saga set back criminal justice reform
  • Personal finance class should be required in high school
  • Distinguished pol of the week: He called out one of the GOP’s dumbest ideas on guns
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 12 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-12 12:50PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Nearly 1 in 4 young Hongkongers from low-income families ‘lying flat’, while some feel hopeless about future, survey shows
  • Hong Kong officials should avoid bulldozing bills through Legislative Council in response to political overhaul, top adviser to city leader warns
  • Coronavirus: infections top 800 for second straight day in Hong Kong, while imported cases surpass 100 for first time
  • Carrie Lam a fighter to the end as she winds down regret-free chief executive tenure
  • Second batch of Hong Kong consumption vouchers to be disbursed during summer, finance chief says, with more details to come this week
  • Hong Kong’s Carrie Lam urges next leadership to toughen up for ‘political struggles’ with foreign critics
  • Hong Kong police charge four suspects in connection with shooting during gang skirmish in Central
  • Unesco-awarded Tai O Heritage Hotel in the ‘Venice of Hong Kong’ unveils facelift in push for eco-tourism and community ties
  • Will Beijing resume dialogue with Hong Kong’s opposition camp? Ex-Legco president Jasper Tsang says this will signal success of ‘one country, two systems’
  • In a Hong Kong led by an ex-police officer, elite administrative officers will still play a major role
  • Hong Kong FCC axed human rights press awards to avoid risk of jail, chief cites concerns about independence of courts under national security law
  • DAB vice-chairman Horace Cheung likely to be picked as Hong Kong’s first deputy secretary for justice
  • Almost 40 per cent of Hong Kong’s outsourced government workers unaware of labour rights, survey finds
  • Hong Kong’s finance chief warns against suspected phishing scam after his picture appears in online adverts
  • Japan calls for ‘self-restraint’ from China after joint fly-past with Russian fighter jets
  • Taiwan’s health minister tests positive for Covid as new cases fall
  • US Navy building its ‘largest, most advanced’ nuclear sub, as Chinese fleet expands
  • To exit its zero-Covid strategy, China needs to build trust in vaccines through more transparency
  • Chinese police arrest 9 men over attack on women in Tangshan
  • What is on the agenda at the WTO’s 12th Ministerial Conference in Geneva?
  • Covid-19 cases rise in China, as ‘ferocious’ Beijing bar cluster and Inner Mongolia trigger worries
  • China’s Africa affairs chief to visit debt-hit Zambia days after Xi Jinping call with president
  • We will fight to the very end, Chinese defence chief warns on Taiwan independence
  • Why China is likely to focus more on Central Asia as US rivalry intensifies
  • Chinese defence officials take aim at US’ ‘aggressive’ Taiwan-Ukraine comparison
  • US-China talks at Shangri-La Dialogue security summit give Southeast Asia ‘some comfort’, Singapore’s Defence Minister Ng Eng Hen says
  • Shangri-La Dialogue: Fiji says climate change, not conflict, is Asia’s biggest security threat
  • Why Virat Kohli, Anushka Sharma, Genelia D’Souza and other Indian A-listers are endorsing plant-based meat
  • Shangri-La Dialogue: Seoul to ‘dramatically’ enhance defence, work with Japan to deter North Korea, defence minister says
  • Sri Lanka PM Ranil Wickremesinghe open to buying Russian oil, accepting China’s financial help
  • Will Indonesia’s quality tourism plan push out domestic visitors from Borobudur?
  • What’s New Zealand’s plan as superpower rivalry escalates in the Pacific?
  • Are Indonesia, Malaysia and Sri Lanka really ‘victims’ of China’s belt and road ‘debt trap diplomacy’?
  • Asean should have a ‘full-time’ Myanmar special envoy with added powers, Malaysia’s Saifuddin Abdullah says
  • US white supremacists arrested near Idaho gay pride event, say police
  • Ukraine war: Russia’s McDonald’s renamed ahead of reopening: ‘Vkusno i tochka!’
  • Zelensky: No one knows how long war will be but Ukraine defying expectations
  • Ukraine war: Kyiv officials dispute Joe Biden’s claim Zelensky ‘didn’t want to hear’ warnings that Putin would invade
  • President Emmanuel Macron’s absolute majority at risk as France elects new parliament
  • Canada, in a world first, proposes health warnings on every cigarette in bid to curb smoking
  • Ukraine war: Russia said to be using more deadly weapons during invasion
  • Oklahoma attorney general seeks execution dates for 25 death row inmates
  • In Kyiv, EU chief promises a signal on Ukraine’s candidate status bid next week
  • Spain’s Catholics want Rome to consider optional celibacy and women priests
  • On New Mexico visit, Joe Biden faces residents’ fury over government efforts to tackle fires
  • Newly discovered dinosaur was a ‘huge’ 32-ft-long predator with a crocodile face, say scientists
  • Thousands of protesters stream to Washington’s National Mall to demand gun law changes
  • Sanctions-hit Iran signs 20-year cooperation deal with Venezuela; allies vow to resist Washington
  • US judge dismisses rape lawsuit against Cristiano Ronaldo in Las Vegas
  • Nicaragua authorises entry of Russian troops, planes and ships
  • Rescuers in Italy locate 7 bodies at scorched site of helicopter crash
  • Outdoor gear retailer Beneunder sparks ire of Communist Party mouthpiece People’s Daily as it seeks Hong Kong IPO
  • The rise and fall of Secoo: how China’s top luxury retailer fell off the catwalk after glory days of US$140 million Nasdaq IPO
  • Samsung Asset Management launches first blockchain-focused ETF in Hong Kong as technology grows beyond cryptocurrencies
  • The ‘Asean way’ has more to offer Asia than a Nato-like security alliance
  • How pandemic-weary young people are embracing ‘live now, pay later’ finance solutions
  • Almost all of Chinese mainland on board with hydrogen development, as Beijing banks on clean fuel for carbon-neutrality drive
  • South Korea’s 40km trail running race across Seoul’s highest mountains is a journey of exquisite peaks, parks and pain
  • One in five men dies before 65 – don’t be that guy. These Men’s Health Week tips, from taking your pulse to checking for lumps, could save your life
  • Melinda Zeman on her Hong Kong childhood, becoming an Orthodox Jew, and finding a creative outlet in jewellery design
  • Sir Robert Ho Tung, man and money: how a Hong Kong Eurasian built a myth and a fortune, and became the richest person in the territory
  • ‘Freedom swimmers’ from China to Hong Kong who braved deadly waters, and police officers assigned to catch and return them, recall dark days
  • How Bruce Lee’s Way of the Dragon made Chuck Norris a star with their fight in Rome’s Colosseum, and what the film showed about Lee’s directing
  • Don’t be like Johnny Depp and Amber Heard – the signs of a toxic relationship to look out for and when to walk away
  • 6 surprising food facts about Queen Elizabeth, according to a royal chef – Darren McGrady formerly cooked for her and Princess Diana, and revealed her chocolate habit and spoiling of her corgis
  • Where are the original Iron Chefs now? Before Netflix rebooted the 1990s Japanese reality show that inspired Masterchef and Hell’s Kitchen, its star chefs opened restaurants worldwide
  • Inside Jeffree Star’s controversial US$200 million net worth: the YouTuber and vegan beauty mogul bought a yak farm, splurges on hot pink Lamborghinis, fuchsia Hermès Birkins, and owns mega mansions
  • David and Victoria Beckham’s multimillion-dollar property empire: from a stylish Miami penthouse and a country estate near Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, to a mansion in London and homes in Dubai
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

  • Paramedics are ‘leaving in droves’ as ambulance callouts almost double

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Taipei Times: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
